top5Opportunities.statement: Minimize likelihood that guide profile without IH fails to achieve required surface hardness for rated cycle count
Minimize likelihood of corrosion-induced structural failure within first 10 years
Minimize likelihood of fire door seal groove outside EN 1634 intumescent strip compression tolerance
Minimize likelihood of sub-spec surface hardness (<55 HRC) reaching assembly undetected
Minimize likelihood of guide rail surface spalling undetected until roller bearing failure causes door jam

needs.statement: Minimize time to commission new custom guide profile die from CAD to qualified first-article delivery
Minimize likelihood that guide profile without induction hardening fails to achieve required surface hardness for rated cycle count
needs.importanceRationale: Un-hardened C45 guide flanges wear within 20-50K cycles vs 500K+ for IH surfaces

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of unplanned lead-time increase >4 weeks on established replenishment order
needs.importanceRationale: Peak production disruption from supply delay

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of guide profiles with sub-specification surface hardness (<55 HRC) reaching door assembly without detection
needs.importanceRationale: IH to 64 HRC with in-line quality check; un-hardened profiles cause early field failure

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of guide rail surface pitting or spalling undetected until roller failure causes door jam
needs.importanceRationale: Surface defect onset to detection gap in operational door

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of corrosion-induced structural failure of outdoor guide rail section within first 10 years
needs.importanceRationale: Bare steel delivery requires external hot-dip galvanizing; once galvanized, equivalent protection

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of fire-rated door failing EN 1634 certification due to seal groove outside intumescent strip compression tolerance
needs.importanceRationale: Cold-drawn achieves +-0.02 mm on groove dimensions — within +-0.05 mm EN 1634 requirement

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of receiving guide profile batches without EN 10204 3.1 material test report
needs.importanceRationale: Fire-door and pressure-equipment applications require material certificates
